skill.name: ralph-tui-create-beads-rust
skill.description: Convert PRDs into beads-rust tasks for Ralph TUI. The skill creates an epic, child beads, quality gates, and dependencies with the br CLI.
skill.summary: Turn PRDs into ordered beads-rust work items for Ralph TUI execution.
skill.author: subsy

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly legitimate br CLI examples and instructions for creating local beads-rust records. Command execution is a real capability, but the skill explicitly uses single-quoted heredocs to reduce shell injection risk. Weak cryptography and SQLite detections appear to be false positives from prose and storage documentation.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Is Core Behavior
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: the skill instructs agents to run br create, br dep add, br sync, and ralph-tui commands. This is expected for the task, but PRD-derived descriptions flow into shell command examples and require careful quoting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct evidence of external command execution. The risk is moderated because the documented heredoc delimiter is single quoted.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the flagged lines contain prose, markdown examples, and description fields. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, key handling, or encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context shows no cryptographic operation on these lines. The scanner likely matched substrings inside markdown text and option names.

low_findings.title: SQLite Database Mention Is Expected Local Storage
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the skill states that beads-rust stores work items under .beads as a SQLite database and JSONL export. No embedded database, credential, or secret value is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines document normal beads-rust storage behavior. I did not find evidence of sensitive data included in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution With Heredoc Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses command substitution with cat heredocs for br descriptions. This pattern can be dangerous if the delimiter is changed or user text is interpolated outside the quoted heredoc.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and handles PRD-derived text. The skill also documents the main mitigation by requiring a single-quoted EOF delimiter.

content.user_title: Convert PRDs into Ralph TUI Beads
content.value_statement: PRDs often need manual cleanup before agents can execute them as small tasks. This skill turns PRDs into ordered beads-rust work with quality gates and dependencies.
actual_capabilities: Extracts quality gates from a PRD and appends them to task acceptance criteria.
Creates an epic bead for the feature using the beads-rust br CLI.
Creates child beads for each user story in the PRD.
Orders stories by dependency so Ralph TUI can execute foundation work first.
Adds br dependency links between beads after task creation.
Runs br sync instructions to export bead data for version control.
limitations: Requires beads-rust and the br CLI to be installed in the workspace.
Works best with PRDs that already contain clear user stories.
May need user input when quality gates are missing from the PRD.
Does not validate that generated tasks compile or pass tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Work From a PRD
use_cases.description: Convert a product requirements document into small beads that Ralph TUI can execute in order.

use_cases.title: Add Quality Gates to Implementation Tasks
use_cases.description: Carry lint, typecheck, and browser verification requirements into every generated user story.

use_cases.title: Build Dependency-Aware Task Plans
use_cases.description: Create schema, backend, UI, and integration work items with clear br dependency links.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Simple PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into beads-rust tasks for Ralph TUI. Ask me for quality gates if they are missing.

prompt_templates.title: Create Beads From a File
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the PRD at tasks/feature-prd.md and create an epic plus child beads with br.

prompt_templates.title: Split Large Stories
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into Ralph TUI beads, splitting any story that is too large for one agent iteration.

prompt_templates.title: Create Ordered Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create beads from this PRD, order schema before backend and UI, then add br dependency links.

output_examples.input: A PRD with three user stories and lint plus typecheck quality gates.
output: An epic bead for the feature.
Three child beads with story-specific acceptance criteria.
Quality gates appended to every child bead.
Dependency links that place backend and UI work after schema work.

output_examples.input: A PRD with one broad dashboard feature and no quality gates.
output: A request for missing quality gate commands.
Several smaller implementation beads for data, server logic, UI, and summary views.
A final sync step so generated bead data can be tracked.

best_practices: Review the PRD for clear user stories before running the skill.
Keep each generated bead small enough for one Ralph TUI iteration.
Use single-quoted heredocs when inserting PRD text into br descriptions.
anti_patterns: Do not create one bead for a whole feature with many unrelated changes.
Do not place UI tasks before their schema or backend dependencies.
Do not remove quality gates from child bead acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Does this skill require beads-rust?
faq.answer: Yes. It is written for the br CLI from beads-rust.

faq.question: Can it use the original beads CLI?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to use a separate skill for the bd CLI.

faq.question: What does it create?
faq.answer: It creates one epic bead, child beads for stories, dependency links, and sync instructions.

faq.question: How does it handle quality gates?
faq.answer: It extracts PRD quality gates and appends them to each child bead acceptance criteria.

faq.question: What happens if quality gates are missing?
faq.answer: It should ask the user for commands or use a sensible default.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because external CLI commands are core behavior.
